description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a robust support structure. The panels are elevated above a platform that appears to house electrical and mechanical components, possibly including inverters and control units. The base platform is detailed with geometric patterns and includes several box-like enclosures and technical modules, suggesting utility or maintenance access. The support structure for the solar panels consists of angled metal beams, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight capture. The installation is designed for urban environments, likely serving as a power generation hub for nearby buildings or public spaces. The overall construction uses a combination of metal, glass, and composite materials, with a focus on durability and efficiency.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a sturdy metal platform with technical enclosures and patterned base, designed for urban solar power generation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the patterned base, technical enclosures, and the full width of the solar panel array above.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled metal support beams and the depth of the platform beneath the solar panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the technical enclosures and the underside of the solar panel array.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional technical modules and the structural supports for the solar panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on angled steel posts with a metal platform base
attribute_array_configuration: single row of approximately 6 large panels arranged side by side
